zh.of, Woodham Walter, Essex, England: 英國英格蘭艾可武德哈姆 沃爾特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Robert FitzWalter於1167年出生在英國英格蘭艾可蕾托 登莫福時，他的父親Walter FitzRobert 2nd Lord of Little Dunmow43歲，母親Mathilda de Lucy31歲。他於1180年與Gunnora de Valognes在英國英格蘭艾可蕾托 登莫福結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1235年12月9日在英國艾可蕾托 登莫福享年68歲，埋葬在英國艾可蕾托 登莫福。

nameMeanings.name: FitzWalter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , ‘son of Walter’.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Walter
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Robert FitzWalter was born in 1167, in Little Dunmow, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, Walter FitzRobert 2nd Lord of Little Dunmow, was 43 and his mother, Mathilda de Lucy, was 31. He married Gunnora de Valognes in 1180, in Little Dunmow, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 9 December 1235, in Little Dunmow, Essex, England, at the age of 68, and was buried in Little Dunmow, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord Robert FitzWalter est né en 1167, à Little Dunmow, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Walter FitzRobert 2nd Lord of Little Dunmow, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Mathilda de Lucy, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Gunnora de Valognes en 1180, à Little Dunmow, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 9 décembre 1235, à Little Dunmow, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Little Dunmow, Essex, Angleterre.

Er hat 1180 in Little Dunmow, Essex, England, United Kingdom Gunnora de Valognes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 9. ja.lifeSummary: Lord Robert FitzWalterが1167年にイギリスイギリスエセックスリットル ダンモウで生まれた時，父Walter FitzRobert 2nd Lord of Little Dunmowは43歳，母Mathilda de Lucyは31歳であった。1180年にイギリスイギリスエセックスリットル ダンモウでGunnora de Valognesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1235年12月9日にイギリスエセックスリットル ダンモウで68歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスリットル ダンモウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Robert FitzWalter(이)가 1167년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 리틀 던모에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter FitzRobert 2nd Lord of Little Dunmow(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilda de Lucy(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1180년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 리틀 던모에서 Gunnora de Valognes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1235년 12월 9일 영국 에세스 리틀 던모에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 리틀 던모에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Robert FitzWalter родился в 1167 г. в местности литл дунмоу, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Walter FitzRobert 2nd Lord of Little Dunmow было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Mathilda de Lucy, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Gunnora de Valognes в 1180 г. в местности литл дунмоу, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 9 декабря 1235 г. в местности литл дунмоу, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности литл дунмоу, эссекс, Англия.
